title: A Moment
content:
Sometimes it all comes running back to me
in odd ways, in odd moments
and it whisks me back to the lilac-blue sky
where I lay wrapped in a blanket
the universe upside down
its contents released
mingling with the chaos outside
and it's almost palpable
the warmth of that cold chilly night
as I saw the moon lighting up the way
for the dancing waves to touch the stars

title: Autumn Leaves
content:
Dry- crippled leaves swing from above
Cradled by the strong-cold wind
And I await their arrival patiently
Skipping on this plush auburn carpet
Timing the rhythm of its crunch
One step here, two steps there
Stop for three-four-five
jump a six
kicking the seventh
and as the red-brown leaves lift above
the dust rises along with it
spiraling upwards to sit atop the worn branches
echoing the voices of the dead
beckoning to the others.
mesmerized by the voices of their ancestral sirens
they dance towards their death
I step aside
For them to join their fallen brethren
And as they touch the ground
They let out a collective sigh
Contently settling down
Waiting to turn into dust.

title: Pains of Growing Up
content:
Lately, it has become much harder to keep my head in the clouds, to keep faith in fantasy
to just eat juicy peaches, laying on the grass and let it be enough
to rest my head on the wall , looking at the stormy clouds and let it be enough
growing up, Enid never seemed to talk about growing up
for her- drinking ginger ale by the seaside
and eating a can of tomatoes was always enough
so i did just that
singing in the rain
dancing in the sea
frolicking in the wind
reaching for the highest tree
talking to the hens
looking for fairies
on top of a hill
rolling on the grass
grabbing a handful of stars
but now
with each passing year
it's becoming difficult to stay up there
alone, in the wooden cabin up the hill
for i can see through the glass
a hustling, bustling city
the road paving its way
to yet another mountain
tearing it apart
forcing me to fend for my worth
or get buried
under the upturned dirt.

title: Gloomy Skies
content:
I sit on a cloud
saving you a seat
let's watch the world unravel
you and me
cracked skies
we'll mend the broken parts
unbroken mirage
of what it could be
and when the world is washed up
dancing and singing in its glory
skies starting to clear out
we'll hurtle down
wrecked hearts drowning
under the plenished sea.

title: 'Te' to my 'Ti'- My Chatto
content:
An year gone
maybe you'll come back in the next one
I still don't get used to it
when she doesn't pass the call
to your rumbling laughter
while you sing my name again and again
love pouring through
telling me a million stories in vivid detail
we have that in common
and our sweet tooth
and the head jiggle
and our faraway souls
or so I would like to believe
I know you're in papa
the same hair, the same rugged hands
the same kindness, the same love
you're in me too
but I'd rather you come back
because it's tiring
to keep finding all of you, everywhere.

title: December 2018
content:
The car turned right from the busy highway into a narrow 'gali' and stopped in front of a familiar redbrick wall.This wall has been here for as long as i can remember .Just one huge wall of bright red colour bricks new as ever, never painted.That wall always fills me with a bitter-sweet feeling, for it means that we have arrived to that house, that we have arrived to this very place.The place that I hated but its people who I loved, and so after 3 years that familiar feeling engulfs me and i move ahead past that beautiful white temple-through that one narrow stairway- and the house of my favourite 'dadi' to the cream painted one with the huge brown door.
And as always there they are, all of them -waiting with bright smiles but my eyes search for my 'chatto' .I run up and hug his huge pillow-y belly and he lets out a hearty laugh , eyes crinkling with happiness , hugging me tightly -letting out a string of endearments I find hard to keep up with. My heart swells with such love for this old man , for he is beautiful, he is one of my favourite people in the whole wide world -my babaji (dadaji), and in that moment I know as i do every time I see him , that as long as he is here, this place is worth coming back to.

title: Life Productions Presents: Tomorrow
content:
I think i'll sit down for a while
Drinking the light in
As the sun goes down
For the cold winds are blowing in
Chilling my bones
Gray haze settling
Draping the mountains
Slowly, drawing in
Sigh, a collective relief
Another play coming to an end.
Lights switched off
I push my seat back
Worn eyes,
Connecting the twinkling lights above
Wondering if I've enough left
To buy the ticket to the next one.

title: Catching Up With Time
content:
Sometimes i am reminded of the beautiful ,little things in life
those things which make you feel human,
those things which make this long
excruciating journey worth travelling.
Be it the memory filled with the fragrance of my youth,
where a bite of the sweet juicy apple
takes me back to the roar of the rushing river
gushing my sorrows and troubles away
and those tall adamant trees-ever changing from
green to yellow to brown to black to white season  after season
telling me that time moves on even if i can't.
But as much as i would like to reminisce on that sweet, carefree past ,
the present is filled with new heart throbbing moments with a hint of nostalgia and pain
be it the day when you ran and flew without a care in the world
like that little child of your past with a friend
who you now have deep-long conversations with and
who makes you realize that there is so much more to you
Those early hours in the morning
where you moved your head and body
to the rhythm that you had long forgotten
or the night
when you had curled up in a blanket beneath the stars
to catch a glimpse of the moon
under the smoky city lights
the same city where in you turn around that corner
to watch the boy you love
and the beautiful pain which makes your chest hurt
as he remains oblivious to it
just as the moon remained hidden that night
in dark-of your existence
its the same pain when you realize
that you've grown way too much
to fly innocently like that little child
who lived near that river-
the river who too had moved on with the times
and the trees which grew around it had shed their leaves
season after season while you were away,
and in the moment you feel so magnificently huge
in the little world of yours
but so worthless as you realize
that you're just a slave of time
and that you and your world will keep on changing and moving
and this right now will just be  a memory
and when you finally look back
it would have already forgotten you.

title: Escape
content:
Palm trees, green leaves flapping in the sky
Yellow-orange light fading in the night
Gentle-strumming
The nimble fingers work
One lighted stick
Deliberately balanced
She gazes at the far horizon
The distant line flickering
Distracted-
Flicking ash onto the sand underneath
She sighs
And softly sings a fine melody
The world comes alive
Captivated by her cheer
Twigs drumming
Bells tinkling
Crabs whispering
The breeze-setting the tempo
whooshing and swishing.
Up there , the moon rises
So does the tide
Its volume increasing
The pebble playing the keys of its water
And her head spins
From the joy of it all
Toes curling-digging into the seashore
She stands and twirls
Gleefully singing, leisurely moving
Still strumming
Soul submerging headfirst into the music
As the wind leads the way
The earth guiding her feet
Crawling in and out from underneath
Steering her deeper into the sea
The blue glass mirroring the night
She touches the stars
Plunging into the ripples it makes
And dances off to the unknown

title: Woeful Waters
content:
The waters of my sorrow are held back by a very delicate barrage
It's crumbly walls swaying to the highs and lows of the tide
I fear the days it breaks
For it engulfs me
It consumes me
And its cords wrap around me so tightly that I suffocate  and am left gasping for breath.
The tributaries of its icy cold water run in my veins and pools in my heart until its heavy
Unable to bear the weight of it.
It then from time to time douses me from head to toe till I shiver , my teeth chatter and I long for warmth
But my cold touch freezes up this world
And the only solace I find is in my sleep as it takes me away from here
For all you give me is pain after pain but my dreams are perfect.
The void is perfect.
It's emptiness-calming.
But it's morning again and not the beautiful kind
For I wake up sweating
Knee deep
Sinking in the fenland of pain.
I lie back
Closing my eyes
Ready to jump in that dark abyss
Hoping against hope that the wreckage of time will lead me back to the shore.

title: Moon River
content:
I float on the big crescent
Bobbing my head to its gentle rhythm
As it wades through the warm currents
Its light illuminating the parts of my face
As swiftly as  it changes its phases
And i stay raptured
As somewhere the trumpet tantalizes the ocean with its melody
The waves dance to its music
And i with them
The  music pouring its joy in my crystal heart
Yet everything feels like a glassy mirage
And i fear it might shatter at  the slightest touch
So i sway my hands with caution
And tap my feet with ease
The ripples giving it away
I look up , hoping the magic would last
But it ebbs away from beneath my fingers
The ripples disappear as the light slowly fades away
To reveal the veiled sea of stars underneath.

title: Amy
content:
‘Fill my heart with song
Let me sing forever more’
For forever I long to live
And the melody of my soul
The Dark sirens it resists
A wild soul
And music my spirit
Independent and real
On my terms I live
Grounded yet zealous
I see a way
Write and write with fervour
As the bass picks up
And the piano drawls
And I hear myself reverberate
In me, in these walls
In the bright sunshine
Shining in the moonlight
Everything is alright
‘I’d gladly surrender my body and soul’
‘For my words are for nothing’
And I just keep wasting my breath
Self esteem in ruins
The bones of my ribs showing
I drown in the shallow
Dragging in myself deeper
As uncountable bottles I swallow
I forget, I stumble, I fight
Dependent, unable to think
I stand on the stage, no music
I did not sign up for this
No help
My hands only weigh me down
The love-bawling on the ground
I pick the nails of my coffin
The smoke- glazing my eyes
Filling my ears
For I am unable to see myself
Or hear that tune
‘Amy Amy Amy
Although I’ve been here before’
Alone or otherwise
I’ll wake up no more.

title: Up In The Mountains
content: I still remember that huge square glass window from wherein the moonlight shone in so bright that it lighted up the small wooden cabin ,I see the small wooden dining table- round in shape , its wood coarse and a plate of cheese and bread kept delicately in the centre. I see the old man and the little girl talking to the goat out in the meadow.The goat whose name i do not remember and the girl who i saw grow up and whose youth remains stagnant as i turn the calendar year after year for i do not have any of those brown, crumbly ,sweet smelling pages to turn. I see those bright ,little stars who were given equal importance up in the sky , their beauty incomparable,their passion fierce.Those tall ,green trees which reached up to the moon and which swayed and danced in the breeze day and night.I took them from granted -the stars ,the moon ,those trees, all of it and so did the girl up until she experienced the stifling, choking environment of that many-walled house through which the noise of the traffic pierced in.There were good different experiences too but she was a wild thing.I had my time and now i complete my unknown sentence in the walled and black clouded jail.I long to go back to the place where i left my heart long ago.I dream about it and i try in futile to never wake up from  it.I will go back.i know, as Heidi did and this time i swear i will not take it for granted.
